title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine statistical tools, campaign data, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.
Whether developing dashboards, analyzing campaign performance, or uncovering insights through SEO and web analytics, each project is rooted in turning complex marketing data into clear, actionable strategies.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
What You’ll Find

Campaign Performance Analysis: Evaluating the effectiveness of digital marketing efforts using data.
Data Visualization: Turning raw datasets into intuitive dashboards and reports.
Marketing Mix & Attribution: Exploring how different channels contribute to success.
Customer & Market Insights: Using analytics to better understand user behavior and optimize experiences.

title: Thank you
text: Form submitted.

title: Strategic Marketing
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, and brand management. These examples highlight how I apply leadership, data-informed decision making, and creative direction to drive marketing success.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 1: Strategic Campaign Planning]
In this project, I led the development of a comprehensive marketing campaign for [product, service, or initiative]. From audience segmentation to messaging strategy, I managed the full lifecycle of the campaign, aligning it with business objectives and measurable KPIs.
Skills Demonstrated:

Marketing Strategy: Defined objectives and tactics for maximum impact.
Team Coordination: Oversaw collaboration across creative, data, and channel teams.
Performance Tracking: Implemented real-time metrics and post-campaign analysis.
Brand Positioning: Ensured consistency across all touchpoints.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 2: Brand Audit & Positioning]
This initiative involved conducting a full brand audit for [organization or brand], evaluating its current market perception, competitive landscape, and customer engagement. I synthesized insights into a refreshed brand strategy and visual identity recommendations.
Skills Demonstrated:

Brand Analysis: Audited messaging, tone, and visual identity.
Competitive Benchmarking: Evaluated brand positioning within the market.
Strategic Communication: Developed new value propositions and storytelling elements.
Stakeholder Presentation: Delivered recommendations to senior leadership.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 3: Product Launch Go-to-Market Plan]
For the release of [new product/service], I created a go-to-market (GTM) strategy from scratch. This included customer journey mapping, channel mix planning, and launch timeline coordination.
Skills Demonstrated:

Go-to-Market Strategy: Developed a clear launch roadmap aligned with buyer needs.
Integrated Marketing: Orchestrated email, paid ads, social, and on-site promotions.
Budget Management: Allocated resources efficiently across tactics.
Launch Optimization: Tracked performance and refined tactics in real time.

Below is an embedded demonstration of the interactive report:

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
[Project Title 4: Client Consulting & Campaign Optimization]
I collaborated with a client to assess and optimize their existing marketing efforts. Through audits, persona development, and campaign refinement, I helped improve ROI and audience engagement.
Skills Demonstrated:

Client Consulting: Provided clear insights and strategic guidance.
Persona Development: Built target personas to guide messaging.
Campaign Optimization: Identified and addressed areas of underperformance.
Data-Driven Adjustments: Leveraged analytics to refine tactics.

title: Campaign Insights Dashboard
text:
Overview
This project involved building a dashboard to track and visualize marketing campaign performance. Metrics like click-through rate, conversion rate, and ROI were tracked across channels using R and Tableau.




Learning Outcomes

Visualize and monitor key marketing metrics.

Analyze performance trends across multiple campaigns.

Identify high- and low-performing segments.

Communicate insights through interactive visuals.

Develop data-informed recommendations.




Key Skills Gained

Dashboard development

Campaign analytics

Data wrangling in R

Tableau or R Shiny visualization

Performance measurement

title: Marketing Funnel Attribution Model
text:
Overview
This project developed a marketing attribution model to assign credit to touchpoints throughout the conversion journey. The insights supported channel optimization and budget reallocation.




Learning Outcomes

Track multi-touch customer journeys.

Model attribution using first-touch, last-touch, and linear approaches.

Visualize funnel performance.

Quantify channel impact on conversion.

Guide future campaign investment decisions.




Key Skills Gained

Attribution modeling

Funnel analysis

Data visualization

Campaign performance analysis

R or Python analytics pipelines

title: SEO Benchmarking Analysis
text:
Overview
This project compared SEO performance across competitors, highlighting keyword gaps, backlink profiles, and content effectiveness. Insights were used to guide an improved content and SEO strategy.




Learning Outcomes

Identify keyword opportunities through competitive research.

Track and compare domain authority and page rankings.

Recommend on-page SEO improvements.

Design a data-driven SEO content strategy.

Visualize competitor positioning.




Key Skills Gained

Keyword research

Web scraping & data cleaning

SEO analysis tools

Data storytelling

Strategy development

title: Customer Segmentation Using Clustering
text:
Overview
In this analysis, customer data was segmented using clustering algorithms to identify meaningful groups. These segments informed targeting strategies and personalization efforts.




Learning Outcomes

Clean and prepare customer-level data.

Apply clustering techniques to group similar customers.

Interpret clusters to define marketing personas.

Recommend tailored messaging strategies.

Support targeted campaign development.




Key Skills Gained

K-means clustering

Data preprocessing

Customer profiling

Market segmentation strategy

R or Python for machine learning

title: Social Media Engagement Analysis
text:
Overview
This analysis examined engagement metrics across multiple social media platforms to identify content trends, audience preferences, and posting strategies that drive performance.




Learning Outcomes

Aggregate and clean social media data.

Identify high-performing content formats.

Understand audience behavior and peak times.

Support social strategy with data-backed recommendations.

Visualize results in an executive summary.




Key Skills Gained

Social analytics

Platform metric APIs

Content performance tracking

Audience insights

Strategic planning

Let’s Connect

Welcome to my portfolio.

title: Marketing Projects
text:
Pharmacy Boardshop Influencer Campaign
For this project, my group created an influencer marketing campaign for Pharmacy Boardshop. The campaign focused on increasing brand awareness and visibility among young skaters, streetwear consumers, Gen Z, and Southern California youth. The goal was to keep the brand connected to real skate culture instead of making the campaign feel like generic paid promotion.
The campaign idea was a four week skate video competition on TikTok and Instagram called “Pharmacy Is Earned.” The campaign used influencers to help build awareness, engagement, user-generated content, and community connection.
View Pharmacy Boardshop Influencer Campaign
Skills used:

Influencer marketing strategy
Social media campaign planning
TikTok and Instagram content strategy
KPI planning
Audience targeting
Brand positioning

title: Marketing Projects
text:
Guinness Welcome Back Campaign Analysis
For this project, I analyzed Guinness’s Welcome Back / #LooksLikeGuinness campaign. The campaign stood out because it used simple black and white visuals that reminded people of a Guinness pint without always showing the product directly.
I looked at the campaign overview, audience, message strategy, creative approach, IMC media channels, and overall effectiveness. This project helped me understand how a strong brand identity can make a campaign memorable across different platforms.
View Guinness Campaign Analysis
Skills used:

Brand campaign analysis
IMC strategy
Audience and objective analysis
Message strategy
Creative strategy
Campaign evaluation

title: Marketing Projects
text:
Why These Projects Matter
These projects connect to digital marketing because they show how strategy, creativity, data, and communication work together. In marketing, it’s important to understand the audience, build a clear message, choose the right platforms, and measure whether the campaign is actually working.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Campaign Strategy
A strong marketing campaign needs more than creative ideas. It also needs a clear target audience, a message that fits the brand, and a way to measure whether the campaign worked.
In my projects, I practiced building campaign strategies around:

Target audience
Brand identity
Social media platforms
Campaign objectives
Influencer fit
KPIs and performance goals

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Brand Positioning
Brand positioning is important because it explains how a brand wants to be seen by its audience. For example, in the Pharmacy Boardshop campaign, the goal was to keep the brand connected to authentic skate culture while still increasing visibility through social media.
This helped me understand that marketing should feel natural to the brand. If the campaign feels too forced, people may not trust it.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Integrated Marketing Communication
The Guinness campaign helped me understand how one clear idea can work across different media channels. The campaign used a simple visual concept and repeated it through TV, outdoor ads, online media, and social media.
This showed me that IMC works best when the message stays consistent, but the content still fits each platform.

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Measuring Success
I also practiced thinking about how campaign success can be measured. Some examples of useful marketing KPIs include:

Reach
Impressions
Engagement rate
Follower growth
Website traffic
Promo code usage
Conversion rate

title: Strategic Marketing
text:
Key Takeaway
The biggest thing I learned is that marketing strategy connects creativity with purpose. A campaign should not just look good. It should match the audience, support the brand, and have clear goals that can be measured.

Who the audience was

The campaign showed how simple visuals, emotional messaging, and consistent media channels can work together.

title: Google Analytics
text:
Why Google Analytics Matters
Google Analytics is useful for a professional portfolio because it can show how visitors interact with the website. For example, it could help track whether people view important pages like my projects, dashboard, presentation, or resume.

title: Google Analytics
text:
Metrics That Could Be Tracked
Some useful website metrics include:

Website visits
Popular pages
Traffic sources
User engagement
Clicks on project links
Resume downloads

title: Google Analytics
text:
Portfolio Use
For this project, the Google Analytics section shows that my website is organized with analytics in mind and ready for tracking code when available.

title: Google Analytics
text:
What I Did
While completing the GA4 certificate, I learned how Google Analytics 4 can track website and app activity. I practiced learning about events, conversions, reports, traffic sources, and how analytics can help businesses understand what users are doing.

title: Google Analytics
text:
What I Learned
The biggest thing I learned is that analytics helps turn website activity into useful marketing insights. Instead of only guessing what people are interested in, marketers can use data to see which pages people visit, how users find the website, and what actions they take.
